revision.text:
[[ja:三國第一山新倉富士浅間神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=三國第一山新倉富士浅間神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Chuurei-tou Fujiyoshida 17025277650 c59733d6ba o.jpg|280px|]]
忠霊塔
{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|type=point|zoom=13|frame-width=280|qq=}} |location={{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujiyoshida|en|Fujiyoshida|qid=Q647508|lt=Fujiyoshida|1=Fujiyoshida}} City, Asama 2-4-1 |LatitudeDeg=35|LatitudeMin=30|LatitudeSec=01.4 |LongitudeDeg=138|LongitudeMin=48|LongitudeSec=00.5 |deity=[[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto]], [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]], [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]] |Rank=Former [[Village Shrine]] |Established=[[Keiun]] 3 (705) }} '''Mikuni Daiichi-zan Arakura Fuji Sengen Shrine''' (三國第一山新倉富士浅間神社, ''Sangoku Daiichi-san Arakura Fuji Sengen Jinja'') is a [[shrine]] located in {{ill|Fujiyoshida|en|Fujiyoshida|qid=Q647508|lt=Fujiyoshida|1=Fujiyoshida}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}. Under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a [[Village Shrine]]. Its official religious corporation name in the registry is Fuji Sengen Shrine. It is also known as '''Arakura Fuji Sengen Shrine''' (新倉富士浅間神社, ''Arakura Fuji Sengen Jinja''). == History == On September 9, Keiun 3 (705), the shrine was established as the tutelary deity of Arakura village in Yatsushiro District, Kai Province. In Daidō 2 (807), following a major eruption of Mount Fuji, an imperial envoy visited the shrine on August 22 to conduct a national peace and Fuji fire pacification ritual. At that time, Emperor Heizei bestowed the title "Mikuni Daiichi-zan" (三國第一山) along with an imperial plaque for the grand torii gate, golden offerings, and a sacred mask known as the ''Hama Hōmen'' (Imperial Envoy Mask). This mask is now housed in the Fujiyoshida City Local History Museum.{{Cite web|url=https://www.arakurafujisengen.com/blank-1|title=History of Mikuni Daiichi-zan Arakura Fuji Sengen Shrine|accessdate=2021-02-12|publisher=Mikuni Daiichi-zan Arakura Fuji Sengen Shrine|qq=}} File:Sangokudaiitisan arakura fuji sengen jinja.jpg|Haiden File:Sangokudaiitisan arakura fuji sengen jinja honden.jpg|Honden. Single-bay gabled structure with a karahafu-style porch, constructed by [[Gun'nai Daiku Nakamaya|Gun'nai Daiku Nakamaya]] in the style of the Komuro Sengen Shrine. == The Imperial Title "Mikuni Daiichi-zan" and the Sacred Mountain Mikoshi == The imperial plaque inscribed with the title "Mikuni Daiichi-zan," attributed to Emperor Heizei, is mentioned in the 15th year of Kyōhō (1730) in the "Shinkura Village Kōtanbetsu Demarcation Ledger."{{Cite book|title=Mount Fuji’s Great Torii: Starting Point of the Yoshida Trail|date=2019-07-23|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture Mt. Fuji World Heritage Center|pages=23|qq=}} The ''Oyama Mikoshi'' (sacred mountain mikoshi), carried during the [[Yoshida Fire Festival]], was traditionally prepared in Shinkura Village. Local officials, shrine priests, and parishioners would carry it around the village before heading to Kamiyoshida to participate in the festival of the Suwa Shrine.{{Cite book|title=Mount Fuji, the Kamakura Road, and the Entrance to the Sacred Mountain: Shinkura|date=2020-07-22|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture Mt. Fuji World Heritage Center|pages=23|qq=}} File:Sangokudaiitisan Arakura Fujisengenjinja gaku.jpg|Plaque attributed to Emperor Heizei File:Yoshida Fire Festival Oyama-Mikoshi.JPG|Oyama Mikoshi, once carried from Shinkura Village == Arakura-yama Sengen Park == The shrine precincts are popularly known as [[Arakura-yama Sengen Park]], attracting worshippers and visitors alike. The five-storied pagoda "Chūreitō," a symbol of the area, serves as a war memorial and is famous for offering "Japan’s best view of Mount Fuji." == Annual Rituals == * ''Misogi Sai'' (Clothing Renewal Festival) - A major ritual held once every 60 years. Participants undergo a week of purification rituals, weaving new garments for the enshrined deities. == See Also == * [[Arakura-yama Sengen Park]] * [[Kitaguchi Hongū Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[Komuro Sengen Shrine (Fujiyoshida)|Komuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Yoshida Fire Festival]] * [[Gun'nai Daiku Nakamaya]] == References == {{translated page|ja|三國第一山新倉富士浅間神社|version=103670362|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Arakura Fuji-sengen-jinja}} [[ja:三國第一山新倉富士浅間神社]] {{wikidata link|Q105949368}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujiyoshida]] [[Category:富士吉田市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Asama shrines]]
